topic: Sign-Only Predictions: A Recurring Non-Discriminating Defense Pattern (new sibling subsection in part2)
our_argument_summary: New <details id="sign-only-non-discrimination"> subsection inserted between #axial-symmetry and #cosmological-cluster in part2.html (Self-Contradictions). Argues that WIN-059 (NMP-Siberian deceleration), WIN-060 (SAA west cell drift), WIN-066 (NH heat excess) share a structural defect: when the dome's quantitative derivation fails or is absent, a defender pivots to "we predicted the sign, not the magnitude." The section gives three counters and classifies WIN-058 as adjacent (definitional circularity).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Pure prose insertion in part2.html. The section quotes magnitudes derived elsewhere: WIN-059 Test 6 39.9% error (from dome monitor.py output, verified in WIN-059 detail_evidence), WIN-066 250-800x over-prediction (90-270 W/m^2 inverse-cube projected vs 0.34 W/m^2 observed, verified in WIN-066 detail_evidence: 90/0.34=265, 270/0.34=794).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — prose section, not a code claim.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None in the section itself. The qualitative claim that the Bayesian update from a sign-match is zero or near-zero is acknowledged in EXP-337 anticipated_objections #5 as not formalized.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None for code claims. See holes_found for citation/DOI issues.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Prose subsection, not a WIN with monitor.py automation.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Our subsection, not a dome prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Our argumentative content, not a dome prediction.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Our critique of the dome's lack of derivation.

holes_found.description: FABRICATED CITATION DOI: The new section cites Livermore et al. (2020) via DOI 10.1038/s41586-020-2018-1. This DOI is INCORRECT. WebSearch confirms 10.1038/s41586-020-2018-1 resolves to Kim et al. 2020, Selective loading and processing of prespacers for precise CRISPR adaptation, published in Nature March 2020 (PMID 32076262). The correct DOI for Livermore, Finlay & Bayliff 2020, Recent north magnetic pole acceleration towards Siberia caused by flux lobe elongation, Nature Geoscience 13:387-391, is 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 — which is the DOI already used in WIN-059 detail_evidence. The EXP-337 sources_verified field falsely claims the wrong DOI is already cited in WIN-059 detail prose. This sits in a section ABOUT citation/derivation standards, which makes the error doubly embarrassing.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch part2.html to replace 10.1038/s41586-020-2018-1 with 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 in the sign-only-non-discrimination subsection. Also patch EXP-337 sources_verified for the historical record.

holes_found.description: UNVERIFIED CITATION DOI: The new section cites Finlay et al. (2016) via 10.1038/nature21807 for the CMB-flow-inversion ~0.2°/yr westward drift rate. WebSearch on 10.1038/nature21807 with Finlay/westward drift/CMB does not surface a Finlay paper at this DOI. The known Finlay 2016/2017 papers are: (a) Finlay et al. 2016 CHAOS-6 in Earth, Planets and Space, DOI 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1; (b) Livermore, Hollerbach & Finlay 2017 An accelerating high-latitude jet in Earth's core in Nature Geoscience, DOI 10.1038/ngeo2859. WIN-060 detail_evidence cites Finlay by name but contains NO DOI. EXP-337 sources_verified claim that 10.1038/nature21807 is already cited in WIN-060 detail prose is false.
holes_found.recommendation: Identify the correct Finlay 2016 reference for the cited 0.2°/yr rate and patch the DOI. Candidates: 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 or 10.1038/ngeo2859. Verify the cited paper actually states the 0.2°/yr figure before settling.

holes_found.description: Counter (2) example mismatch with WIN-060: The section examples include a drift rate, a westward shift coordinate, a +0.34 W/m^2/decade. WIN-066 supplies the +0.34 and WIN-059 supplies a drift rate, but WIN-060 detail_evidence states the dome has no derivation from dome geometry or prediction of the drift rate. WIN-060 does NOT cite a specific magnitude. Counter (2) therefore does not apply to WIN-060; Counter (3) covers it. A defender could nitpick the example list as misrepresenting WIN-060.
holes_found.recommendation: Edit Counter (2) example to clarify: e.g., a drift rate (WIN-059) or +0.34 W/m^2/decade (WIN-066); drop WIN-060 from Counter (2) and route it explicitly through Counter (3).

holes_found.description: Continental-area independence claim is partially convention-dependent. The reply states underlying observables (continental land-mass percentages, observatory readings, CERES fluxes) are direct measurements independent of the geometry dispute. Observatory readings and CERES fluxes are direct instrumental quantities; but the ~39% NH vs ~19% SH partition depends on what counts as a hemisphere. On a polar-azimuthal disc, that boundary is conventional. A defender could pivot to: your observable secretly imports the globe-sphere hemisphere convention.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten phrasing to: continental land-mass and ocean distributions, observatory magnetic-field readings, and CERES top-of-atmosphere fluxes — empirical quantities measurable independent of which projection you display them in. Standard models derive magnitudes from these; the dome adopts numbers without re-derivation in disc-frame coordinates.

holes_found.description: WIN-066 dome figure phrasing: The new section uses +0.34 W/m^2/decade while WIN-066 prose writes +0.34 W/m^2 (and notes parenthetically that the underlying Loeb 2025 measurement is per-decade). Pedantic defender could argue we never claimed per decade.
holes_found.recommendation: Standardize across WIN-066 and the new subsection. Stylistic, non-load-bearing.

holes_found.description: TLDR coin-flip metaphor slightly overstates the formal argument. Counter (3) says sign-only has the same falsifiability content as a coin flip relative to alternatives; TLDR phrasing reads as if every defense becomes literally 50/50. The section text gets this right.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: tighten TLDR last sentence to: Each per-WIN sign-match is non-discriminating against standard physics, so the confirmation collapses.

stronger_arguments.description: State the Bayesian point as a likelihood ratio: L = P(sign | dome) / P(sign | standard model) ~ 1, so by Bayes' rule the posterior over the two models is unchanged. This disarms the anticipated curmudgeon objection #5 (strict definition of non-discriminating requires Bayesian formalism) without committing to priors.
sources: Standard Bayesian inference; e.g., MacKay 2003 ITILA Chapter 2.

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-link to Part 6 (Falsifiability) where the post-hoc-prediction standard is defined. The section pivots on whether the dome derives a magnitude — the same question Part 6 anchors. A one-sentence cross-link tightens the network of internal references.
sources: docs/index.html Part 6 / sections.json part6 — verify anchor before patching.

stronger_arguments.description: Name the tolerance widening: WIN-059 prose flags the 50% threshold widened from a tighter value. Saying so in the new section makes the dome's sign-only retreat look like a documented pattern (move goalposts then claim PASS) rather than an abstract logical move.
sources: WIN-059 detail_evidence; Sections 6.5-6.6 (tolerance-gaming and asymmetric scorecard pattern).

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome is doing real triage when it notices NMP drift, SAA westward motion, and NH heat asymmetry are signed, persistent signals worth predicting (EXP-337-cross-win-sign-only-sweep.json:kernel_of_truth). All three are real, well-measured features: Livermore et al. 2020 (Nat Geosci 13:387, doi 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9) documents the NMP/Siberia flux-lobe mechanism quantitatively; Finlay 2016 CHAOS-6 (Earth Planets Space) tabulates SAA westward drift rates; Loeb 2025 PNAS (doi 10.1073/pnas.2511595122) quantifies the NH heat-imbalance trend. The dome was right to identify each as a phenomenon worth claiming, and the new subsection (sections.json:part2 sign-only-non-discrimination) explicitly preserves this credit — signed observations are real and the dome was right to notice them.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Noticing a signed signal is not deriving it. WIN-066 is the test case where the dome DID attempt a derivation — the inverse-cube projected-flux integral over the dome's h = 5,733 km sun altitude — and the answer (90-270 W/m^2, verified in wins.json:WIN-066.detail_evidence) is 264-794x the observed 0.34 W/m^2. WIN-059 has Test 6 at 39.9% error (wins.json:WIN-059.detail_evidence — PASS only by using a 50% tolerance that its own dashboard notes was widened). WIN-060 has no derivation at all (wins.json:WIN-060.detail_evidence — no derivation from dome geometry or prediction of the drift rate). In each case, the standard model produces the magnitude from independent observables; the dome adopts the numbers without re-derivation. A sign-match against the only mechanistic derivation on the table is non-discrimination, exactly as the new subsection argues.

tldr_review.tldr_text: Three V51 confirmations (NMP deceleration sector, SAA westward cell shift, NH heat excess) share a structural defect: when the dome's quantitative derivation fails or is absent, a defender retreats to we predicted the SIGN, not the magnitude. But standard physics independently predicts the same sign through unrelated mechanisms, so a sign-match is non-discriminating between the two models. Each per-WIN defense becomes a coin flip and collapses the confirmation.
tldr_review.notes: TLDR is broadly accurate. The coin-flip simplification in the last clause is slightly stronger than the formal argument; see holes_found minor #6.

cited_dois_issues: s41586-020-2018-1 resolves to a CRISPR paper (Kim et al. 2020), NOT Livermore et al. 2020 NMP paper — correct is 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9
nature21807 unverified for Finlay 2016 CMB flow inversion; likely wrong

deeper_analysis: Cross-WIN holistic section. The structural argument is solid: a sign-match against a model that derives the same sign from independent observables is non-discriminating, and the dome adopts magnitudes from those observables wholesale rather than re-deriving from disc/firmament parameters. The kernel-of-truth treatment is honest, the WIN-058 adjacency is correctly disclosed, and the anticipated counter-pivot is preempted with two reasonable replies. Where the section is exposed is at the citation layer: two of three DOIs are wrong/unverified, and EXP-337 sources_verified field made false claims that both were already in cited WINs. This is the most exploitable hole for a sophisticated defender — your section ABOUT citation standards cites a CRISPR paper for magnetic pole dynamics. Fixing the DOIs (and adding a tests/run.sh check that fails when a section cites a DOI not present in the cited WIN's detail prose) would prevent recurrence. The moderate Counter (2) WIN-060 mismatch is real but cosmetic; Counter (3) covers WIN-060. The land-area-convention nit is technically correct but misses the point; a brief rephrase neutralizes it without losing the argument. Verdict: the new subsection is rhetorically sound and does close WIN-066.c5 hole h3, but the citation errors are critical-tier and should be patched before this becomes the canonical cross-WIN argument other sections cross-reference.

citations_verified: 10.1073/pnas.2511595122 (Loeb et al. 2025) — present in WIN-066 detail_evidence (count=2 in WIN-066 prose); used correctly in new section
Section 2.9 anchor #2-9-the-v13-coordinate-system-a-self-referential-loop — present in part2.html at byte 23870
#axial-symmetry, #sign-only-non-discrimination, #cosmological-cluster anchors all resolve in part2.html (single occurrence each)
citations_failed: WRONG DOI: 10.1038/s41586-020-2018-1 cited as Livermore et al. 2020. Resolves to Kim et al. 2020 CRISPR adaptation paper (PMID 32076262). Correct DOI for the NMP-Siberia paper: 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 (matches WIN-059 detail prose).

UNVERIFIED/LIKELY WRONG DOI: 10.1038/nature21807 cited as Finlay et al. 2016. Not surfaced in WebSearch as a Finlay paper on westward drift / CMB flow inversion. Known Finlay 2016/2017 relevant works: 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 (CHAOS-6) or 10.1038/ngeo2859 (2017 jet paper).
citations_unchecked: 10.1038/ngeo1580 (Stephens et al. 2012) — referenced by section context (not directly linked in new subsection). Already in WIN-066 detail prose (count=2); presumed correct.

summary_for_decider: CRITICAL DOI ERRORS in the new sign-only-non-discrimination subsection. (1) 10.1038/s41586-020-2018-1 cited for Livermore et al. 2020 resolves to an unrelated CRISPR paper; correct DOI is 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 (already used in WIN-059 prose). (2) 10.1038/nature21807 cited for Finlay et al. 2016 is unverified; likely candidates are 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 or 10.1038/ngeo2859. EXP-337 sources_verified field falsely claimed both DOIs were already in cited WIN prose. Patch DOIs immediately. Worth a one-off audit pass over recent analyst EXPs (last ~30 days) for sources_verified accuracy, and worth a tests/run.sh check that fails when a section cites a DOI not also present in the cited WIN's detail prose. Beyond DOI errors, the section is rhetorically solid and does close WIN-066.c5 hole h3. Three moderate/minor cleanups recommended (Counter (2) WIN-060 alignment, land-area independence phrasing, TLDR coin-flip simplification) — non-blocking. Verdict not changed; recommended_action minor_edit. Singleton review (class:holistic — not batchable). queue_id 352 set per PROP-009.
